serving.natural_language_portion: A standard hawker serving of nasi padang with sambal goreng is approximately 300 g, comprising roughly 150–180 g of steamed white rice topped with a portion of sambal goreng (tempeh, long beans, and tofu in spiced sambal sauce).

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Padang (Sambal Goreng) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Nasi Padang (Sambal Goreng), CR ID F05100095. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for nasi padang sambal goreng as a composite dish. GI of 72 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white jasmine rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database). The sambal goreng components (tempeh, long beans, tofu) contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 21.85 × 3.0) / 100 ≈ 37.4 for a 300 g serving (carbohydrate ~65.6 g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 240 mg/100 g from belacan (shrimp paste) and added salt in sambal preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to reduce sodium load and request reduced belacan and salt during preparation; pairing with potassium-rich vegetable sides (e.g., kangkong, spinach) can help partially offset the sodium-to-potassium imbalance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI ~72) contributing 21.85 g carbohydrate per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit the rice portion and increase the proportion of sambal goreng (tempeh, tofu, long beans) to reduce glycemic impact; substituting brown rice or reducing serving size to 150 g rice is advisable to manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 2.5 g/100 g and sodium 240 mg/100 g; tempeh provides cardioprotective isoflavones and dietary fibre 3.13 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size to control sodium and saturated fat intake, while the tempeh and long beans in sambal goreng provide beneficial dietary fibre and plant protein that support lipid management; request reduced oil and belacan in preparation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fermented tempeh provides gut-immune modulating live cultures; dietary fibre 3.13 g/100 g exceeds the 3 g threshold; anti-inflammatory spices present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the fermented tempeh and anti-inflammatory spices (lemongrass, garlic) in this dish; however, those with nightshade sensitivity should monitor their response to chilli, and belacan should be reduced to minimise sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Padang with Sambal Goreng is a popular Malay-Singaporean hawker dish consisting of steamed white jasmine rice served alongside sambal goreng — a spiced stir-fry of tempeh (fermented soybean cake), long beans, and tofu cooked in a fragrant sambal paste made from dried chillies, belacan (shrimp paste), shallots, garlic, and lemongrass. The dish is a staple at nasi padang stalls across Singapore and Malaysia, where diners select from an array of pre-cooked dishes to accompany their rice. The sambal goreng component delivers a bold, savoury-spicy flavour with a slightly smoky depth from the belacan, balanced by the mild nuttiness of tempeh and the tender bite of long beans and tofu. Nutritionally, the dish provides 175 kcal per 100 g, with 6.37 g protein, 7.57 g fat, and 21.85 g carbohydrate. Dietary fibre is notably high at 3.13 g/100 g relative to other nasi padang variants, contributed by tempeh, long beans, and tofu. Sodium is 240 mg/100 g, primarily from belacan and added salt. The dish is halal and gluten-free. Clinically, the high-GI white rice base (GI ~72) makes portion control important for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The fermented tempeh contributes isoflavones and live cultures with potential gut-immune benefits relevant to inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. The dish is classified as a rice-based mixed dish under the Grains and Staples food group.

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical entry F05100095 with confidence 0.95. GI estimated from white jasmine rice analogue via University of Sydney GI Database, which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor. Saturated fat (2.5 g/100 g) and potassium (~190 mg/100 g) are consistent with HPB-reported values referenced in the input health suitability notes. All mandatory multilingual names are present.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Malay rice dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05100095 as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail not available in HPB Foodo for this composite dish entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: no trans fats expected in a traditionally prepared sambal goreng using standard cooking oils; confirmed consistent with HPB data. potassium_mg value of 190 mg/100 g is derived from the input health suitability notes referencing HPB data; this is an approximate value. Saturated fat of 2.5 g/100 g is referenced from the input health suitability notes (HPB-consistent); the HPB Foodo entry does not separately report saturated fat in the per_100g fields provided. Note: this dish contains belacan (shrimp paste) and is therefore NOT suitable for strict vegetarians or vegans despite containing plant-based ingredients; dietary_flags is_vegetarian and is_vegan are set to false accordingly. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white jasmine rice as the dominant carbohydrate source per University of Sydney GI Database.
